title: Buttery Juicy Peruvian Cashew Stew
description: A creamy, pressure‑cooked stew that blends buttery cashews with earthy sweet potatoes and carrots, finished with a bright lime‑cilantro flourish—perfect for a lazy Sunday and friendly to diabetic hearts.

ingredients.name: raw cashews
ingredients.notes: soaked 4 hours or overnight and drained

instructions: Soak the cashews in warm water for 4 hours or overnight; drain and set aside.
Heat the coconut oil in the pressure cooker on the sauté setting and brown the diced onion for 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Stir in the diced sweet potatoes, carrots, cumin, and smoked paprika; cook for 2 minutes to toast the spices.
Pour in the vegetable broth and add the soaked cashews.
Seal the pressure cooker and cook on high pressure for 5 minutes.
Release the pressure manually, then stir the stew; it will be silky and loose—add extra broth if needed to thin.
If desired, stir in chopped cilantro and lime juice for brightness; season with a pinch of sea salt.
Serve hot over a small portion of quinoa or plain rice to keep the carb count down.
Enjoy the creamy, buttery flavor balanced with fresh citrus notes.
